title: WordPress Customizer Manager Setup

activity_flow.description: Initialize manager with changeset_uuid, theme, messenger_channel, settings_previewed, autosaved, branching
decisions: If changeset_uuid not provided → generate via wp_generate_uuid4()
If theme not in args → read from $_REQUEST['customize_theme'] or $_REQUEST['theme']
If messenger_channel not in args → read from $_REQUEST['customize_messenger_channel']
side_effects: Load component class files (setting, panel, section, control, and all specializations)
Apply filter 'customize_loaded_components' to determine active components
Register AJAX handlers: customize_save, customize_trash, customize_refresh_nonces, customize_load_themes, etc.

activity_flow.description: Permission checks, nonce validation, theme validation, kick off changeset establishment and preview
decisions: If pagenow==customize.php AND user cannot 'customize' → auth_redirect() or wp_die(403)
If changeset_uuid set but invalid UUID format → wp_die(-1, 'Invalid changeset UUID')
Check CSRF nonce (preview-customize_, save-customize_, customize_preview_nonce); if fails → clear POST/REQUEST customized data
If user cannot 'customize' AND no changeset_post_id → wp_die(-1 or 0 for messenger)
If is_theme_active() → add after_setup_theme → after_setup_theme()
Else (different theme): check switch_themes cap, theme errors, multisite allowed; fail → wp_die()
If fresh_site AND customize.php → schedule import_theme_starter_content at after_setup_theme:100

activity_flow.description: Determines which changeset UUID to use for this session
decisions: If _changeset_uuid already set → skip UUID resolution
If branching disabled AND theme is active → query most recent unpublished changeset post (get_changeset_posts, status not in auto-draft/publish/trash/inherit/private, newest first)
If found and valid UUID → reuse that changeset UUID
Else → generate new UUID via wp_generate_uuid4()
If branching enabled → always generate new UUID
If is_admin AND pagenow==customize.php → set_changeset_lock(changeset_post_id())

activity_flow.activity: after_setup_theme()  [fires on after_setup_theme, active-theme path only]
decisions: If not doing AJAX AND not customized POST data AND validate_current_theme() fails → wp_redirect('themes.php?broken=true') + exit
If already previewing (is_preview()) → bail (no-op)
Set $this->previewing = true
If NOT active theme → add_filter for template, stylesheet, pre_option_current_theme, pre_option_stylesheet, pre_option_template, pre_option_stylesheet_root, pre_option_template_root
If IS active theme → no filters needed

activity_flow.description: Returns $this->settings_previewed boolean — controls whether WP_Customize_Setting::preview() is applied

decisions: If not previewing → bail (no-op)
Set $this->previewing = false
If NOT active theme → remove all template/stylesheet filters added in start_previewing_theme()

activity_flow.description: autosaved() returns bool for loading autosave revision; branching() applies 'customize_changeset_branching' filter and returns bool
decisions: branching() applies filter 'customize_changeset_branching' before returning — allows external override

key_conditions: User capability 'customize' required for customize.php access
User capability 'switch_themes' required for non-active theme preview
Valid UUID format required for changeset_uuid
CSRF nonce checked via check_ajax_referer; failure strips POST customized data
Changeset branching (default false) controls whether existing draft changesets are reused
Fresh site flag triggers starter content import on first customize.php load
messenger_channel presence controls admin bar visibility and error messaging mode
important_notes: Changeset = a CPT post (post_name = UUID) storing pending customization data. Branching=false (default) means linear single-changeset mode; all users share one draft. Branching=true means each user/session gets an independent changeset. settings_previewed=true (default) means WP_Customize_Setting::preview() is called to apply pending values in the preview iframe. autosaved=true loads from the autosave revision of the changeset post rather than the published post content.
